svg.title: Character Connection Graph
svg.desc: Auto-generated graph of connected characters from the murder mystery character briefs.
svg.text: Generated from the Connections sections in character-brief source files.
Assigned role referenced without a brief

line.title: Agent Red -> Agent Blue (<played by>) - You know Blue only through compartmented tradecraft, a handoff protocol, and the expectation that Blue will tell you who Moscow has marked for death.

Agent Red -> Victor Hale (<played by>) - Your cover, your access point, and a man who has told you far more than he realizes. He sees you as polished company. You see every crack in the mask. | Victor Hale -> Your new girlfriend (<played by>) - Elegant, socially fluent, and a surprisingly natural fit for rooms like this one. You enjoy what she suggests about your taste, your relevance, and your future.
Agent Red -> Charles Whitmore (<played by>) - The guest of honor. Victor has spoken of him often enough that you know where the diplomacy ends and the procurement begins.

Agent Red -> The SCSR recipient (<played by>) - You do not know who they are openly. You know only the recognition signal, the cipher key in your possession, and that Moscow considers them worth the risk. | Talia Jane -> Two KGB agents will be in attendance. For everyone's protection, you don't know them directly, but they are known as Agent Red and Agent Blue.
Eleanor Mercer -> Graham Voss (<played by>) - You don't know him, but he's an insane conspiracy theorist who claims to be former CIA and cannot be trusted.

Eleanor Mercer -> The President (<played by>) - Your husband. The country sees a partnership. You see a man you have carried for years and intend to leave the moment it is safe to do so. | President Daniel Mercer -> The First Lady (<played by>) - Your wife and most reliable political instinct. She reads rooms faster than any chief of staff you have ever employed.
Eleanor Mercer -> Sam Jackson (<played by>) - A respected columnist whose interest could make your book seem thoughtful, serious, and worth discussing in the right circles.
Eleanor Mercer -> Karl Franks (<played by>) - A young journalist with an appetite for scandal. If handled carefully, that appetite might be redirected toward curiosity.
Eleanor Mercer -> Charles Whitmore (<played by>) - A brilliant diplomat with a dangerous weakness for pageantry and self-mythology. Fortunately, pageantry attracts press.
Eleanor Mercer -> Anna Whitmore (<played by>) - A gifted hostess and musician whose poise is too deliberate tonight to be entirely natural.
Thing 1 -> The First Lady (<played by>) - A secondary protectee whose social instincts often run directly against your perimeter plan.

Eleanor Mercer -> Thing 2 Secret Service (<played by>) - The agent assigned closest to you this evening. He treats you like a person, not a porcelain figurine, which you appreciate. | Thing 2 -> The First Lady (<played by>) - Your principal for the evening. She likes that you talk to her like a person instead of a moving security problem.
President Daniel Mercer -> Graham Voss (<played by>) - You don't know him, but he's an insane conspiracy theorist who claims to be former CIA and cannot be trusted.
Sam Jackson -> Graham Voss (<played by>) - You don't know him, but he's an insane conspiracy theorist who claims to be former CIA and cannot be trusted.
Victor Hale -> Graham Voss (<played by>) - You don't know him, but he's an insane conspiracy theorist who claims to be former CIA and cannot be trusted.

Graham Voss -> Karl Franks (<played by>) - The younger reporter sent because he looks legitimate and you do not. He may be a tool, a rival, or proof that someone is steering the story. | Karl Franks -> Graham Voss (<played by>) - You don't know him, but he's an insane conspiracy theorist who claims to be former CIA and cannot be trusted.

Graham Voss -> Charles Whitmore (<played by>) - A polished statesman who has spent years translating corruption into respectable language. | Charles Whitmore -> Graham Voss (<played by>) - You don't know him, but he's an insane conspiracy theorist who claims to be former CIA and cannot be trusted.

Graham Voss -> Talia Jane (<played by>) - Too polished, too protected, and too often near the sort of private conversations that never make it into official minutes. | Talia Jane -> Graham Voss (<played by>) — You don't know him, but he's an insane conspiracy theorist who claims to be former CIA and cannot be trusted.

Graham Voss -> Anna Whitmore (<played by>) - You once got too close while investigating her husband's world. She knows you are not as mad as people say, which makes her dangerous. | Anna Whitmore -> Graham Voss (<played by>) - You don't know him, but he's an insane conspiracy theorist who claims to be former CIA and cannot be trusted.
Thing 1 -> Graham Voss (<played by>) - A former CIA operative turned journalist. Maybe unstable, maybe opportunistic, but people with his background do not get the benefit of the doubt from you.
Thing 2 -> Graham Voss (<played by>) - You don't know him, but he's an insane conspiracy theorist who claims to be former CIA and cannot be trusted.
Charles Whitmore -> Josh Roberson from the New York Times who you haven’t seen in many years
President Daniel Mercer -> Victor Hale (<played by>) - A defense executive who never asks for anything without pretending it is in the national interest.
President Daniel Mercer -> Charles Whitmore (<played by>) - A diplomat who delivered results, blurred lines, and now wants history to remember only the first part.
President Daniel Mercer -> Talia Jane (<played by>) - The heir apparent in diplomatic circles. Talented enough to promote, ambitious enough to watch carefully.

President Daniel Mercer -> Thing 1 Secret Service (<played by>) - The more rigid, junior half of your detail. Competent, severe, and still not entirely reconciled to Marcus outranking him. | Thing 1 -> The President (<played by>) - Your principal. Important enough to protect, careless enough to make that difficult, and somehow still convinced Marcus should be the senior agent on this detail.

President Daniel Mercer -> Thing 2 Secret Service (<played by>) - The lead agent on your detail tonight. Easier to underestimate, which is exactly why he is effective. | Thing 2 -> The President (<played by>) - Your principal for the evening. He prefers a calm room, which is one reason you keep getting the lead.

Sam Jackson -> Karl Franks (<played by>) — A journalist you know about professionally, though he might not know of you. Ambitious. Sharp. Capable of shaping a narrative quickly if given the opportunity.

Sam Jackson -> Charles Whitmore (<played by>) — Your lifelong best friend. You have defended him publicly and privately for decades. | Charles Whitmore -> Sam Jackson from the Greenwich Local - he is also your closest friend since childhood - as close as he is you have never told him you secret

Sam Jackson -> Alice Harden (<played by>) - Anna’s best friend who is an extremely talented harpist and has always been kind to you. | Alice Harden -> Sam Jackson (<played by>) - Charles’s childhood best friend, an all around stand up guy.
Talia Jane -> Sam Jackson (<played by>) — A respected columnist whose opinion can legitimize your rise if he trusts your character.

Sam Jackson -> Anna Whitmore (<played by>) — Charles’s wife. You respect her talent and her poise, and you have watched their marriage endure the pressures of public life. | Anna Whitmore -> Charles’ childhood best friend, Sam Jackson (<played by>). You think the world of Sam, he’s a true gentlemen and a man of principle.
Victor Hale -> Jack Grace (<played by>) - A quiet Aegis engineer in your orbit. Competent, forgettable, and useful in rooms where the technical men outnumber the political ones.
Talia Jane -> Two KGB agents will be in attendance. For everyone's protection, you don't know them directly, but they are known as Agent Red and Agent Blue.
Victor Hale -> Karl Franks (<played by>) - A dangerous young journalist who would happily burn a career or a company if it put his byline on the front page.
Victor Hale -> Charles Whitmore (<played by>) - A longtime political partner who understands that diplomacy without industry is just theater.
Victor Hale -> Talia Jane (<played by>) - The incoming diplomat. Ambitious, polished, and not yet as independent as she wants people to think.
Victor Hale -> Anna Whitmore (<played by>) - A woman who understands the value of appearances and the cost of a badly timed scandal.
Thing 1 -> Victor Hale (<played by>) - A man who thinks money should purchase flexibility. You suspect he has never once made a security problem smaller, but tonight your attention is elsewhere.
Thing 2 -> Victor Hale (<played by>) - Smooth, connected, and exactly the kind of guest who causes procedural headaches while calling them misunderstandings.

Sam Grossman -> The Journalist (<played by>) — The man you saw with Anna in London — or someone connected to him. If he starts asking the right questions, you must subtly encourage him… without ever revealing how you know.
Talia Jane -> Karl Franks (<played by>) — A volatile journalist with the gaul and ambition to expose you if that's what benefits him.
Thing 2 -> Karl Franks (<played by>) - A young reporter with the expression of a man who thinks scandal is a public service.

Charles Whitmore -> The director of the London Symphony Orchestra, Sam Grossman (<played by>). You have made big donations to his organization - Grossman being here will be a good look. It’s a little uncomfortable though because he has always been in love with Anna. Poor guy. | Sam Grossman -> Charles Whitmore (<played by>) — A powerful patron and a man you have worked alongside for years. His donation reshaped your orchestra. He trusts you — perhaps too much.

Alice Harden -> Charles Whitmore (<played by>) — You respect his achievements and understand the power structures he navigates. You know more about his political alliances than most guests in the room.
Maggie Bell -> Charles Whitmore (<played by>) - The man who opened a golden door for Anna and quietly shut plenty of others behind her.

Charles Whitmore -> The ambassador taking over your role, Talia Jane (played by …). She is young and looks up to you, but doesn’t yet understand the inner workings of the political space. And she definitely doesn’t know your secrets. | Talia Jane -> Charles Whitmore (<played by>) — Your predecessor and public sponsor. He believes you are a safe continuation of his legacy.
Anna Whitmore -> Your husband, Charles Whitmore (<played by>)
Thing 1 -> Charles Whitmore (<played by>) - The host. Charming, self-important, and allergic to the phrase "for security reasons."

Sam Grossman -> Alice Harden (<played by>) — A supremely talented harpist who may suspect favoritism. If Anna falls, Alice rises. You must determine whether she is an ally, a threat, or an opportunity. | Alice Harden -> If you’re right, Sam Grossman of the London Symphony Orchestra (<played by>) will be in attendance.
Maggie Bell -> Sam Grossman (<played by>) - The orchestra man who worships Anna from a tasteful distance and has always looked at you like you might say the wrong thing at the right time.
Sam Grossman -> Anna Whitmore (<played by>) — The woman you love. You elevated her career and protected her position. She does not know you saw her betrayal.
Maggie Bell -> Alice Harden (<played by>) - One of the only people in Anna's orbit who has ever treated you like a human being instead of a complication.

Alice Harden -> Anna Whitmore (<played by>) — Your best friend and musical partner for decades. You have built a life performing beside her, but you are tired of being second. | Anna Whitmore -> Your best friend and second chair harpist throughout your career, Alice Harden (<played by>). You have performed next to her so many times and have such great memories at parties just like this.
Maggie Bell -> Anna Whitmore (<played by>) - Your older sister. You have spent years resenting her, but beneath it lives real admiration for everything she made of herself.
Talia Jane -> Anna Whitmore (<played by>) — She sees you as polished and useful, but she is perceptive enough to notice cracks in your facade.
Thing 2 -> Anna Whitmore (<played by>) - The hostess. Gracious, observant, and much too good at pretending everything is perfectly fine.

Thing 1 -> Thing 2 Secret Service (<played by>) - Your technically superior partner. Capable, observant, and living proof that this administration sometimes mistakes charm for judgment. | Thing 2 -> Thing 1 Secret Service (<played by>) - Your junior partner. A great agent, a terrible audience, and quietly convinced the promotion board experienced temporary madness.
